senses.definition: a wild plant (Centaurea picris) with yellowish flowers that grows among grasses and is considered a weed in some places
examples: The field was overgrown with sheepweed and other weeds.
Farmers weeded the patch to remove sheepweed.

senses.definition: relating to or resembling a sheep; timid or gentle in a way associated with sheep
examples: The child gave the sheepy eyes at the farmer.
The sheepy scent filled the countryside air.

senses.definition: completely or very thin and light; almost vertical or steep
examples: The dress is made of sheer fabric.
The cliff rose at a sheer angle.

senses.definition: absolute or complete; not mixed with anything else
examples: She faced the problem with sheer determination.
It was sheer luck that we arrived on time.

senses.definition: past tense of shear; to cut the wool from a sheep or to cut off or remove something, especially hair or wool
examples: They sheered the sheep in the early morning.
The hedge was sheered back to restore its shape.

senses.definition: a comparative form of sheer; something that is more sheer or transparent
examples: This fabric is a sheerer than the previous one.
Choose a sheerer material for a lighter look.

senses.definition: the most sheer; extremely transparent or pure
examples: The sheerest fabric barely blocked the light.
That line of reasoning is the sheerest nonsense.

senses.definition: the act of cutting the wool from a sheep or trimming edges; the process of making something smooth or straight by cutting off irregularities
examples: Farmers perform sheering annually.
The carpenter started sheering the edge to fit perfectly.

senses.definition: a single long leg on a sailing ship or a structural support that appears as a single slender leg
examples: The mast stood on a stout sheerleg.
They relied on the sheerleg to lift the heavy beam.

senses.definition: plural of sheerleg; multiple single-legged lifting devices or supports
examples: They used several sheerlegs to move the locomotive.
The workshop had two large sheerlegs for heavy lifting.

senses.definition: in a very transparent or completely; utterly
examples: The cliff faced sheerly upward.
She smiled sheerly at the compliment.

senses.definition: the quality of being sheer; transparency or thinness
examples: The sheerness of the fabric was remarkable.
Sheers contributed to the room's bright feel.

senses.definition: plural of sheerness; multiple instances of transparency or thinness
examples: The sheernesses of the curtains varied by light.
They evaluated the sheernesses of several fabrics.

senses.definition: plural of sheer; lightweight, transparent fabric often used for curtains
examples: The sheers let light in softly.
She wore white sheers to the wedding.

senses.definition: present tense or plural form of sheer; to cut or strip away hard edges; to become thin or transparent
examples: The light sheers through the fabric.
They sheers the hedge yearly.

senses.definition: an expression of mild exasperation or surprise
examples: Sheesh, that took forever.
He spilled soda again, sheesh.

senses.definition: a water pipe used for smoking flavored tobacco; also spelled shisha
examples: They smoked apple-flavored sheesha at the cafe.
The sheesha session lasted an hour.

senses.definition: plural of sheesha; multiple water pipes for smoking
examples: The lounge offered several sheeshas for customers.
Two friends shared different flavored sheeshas.

senses.definition: a flat piece of cloth used on a bed
examples: I changed the bed sheet this morning.
The sheet is folded neatly.

senses.definition: a large rectangular piece of paper or material
examples: The treasure map was a fragile sheet.
Write your name on the sheet at the front.

senses.definition: the amount or quality of sheets (paper or fabric) in a batch; a financial or production sheet count
examples: The sheetage of the warehouse was high this quarter.
We checked the sheetage before printing.

senses.definition: covered or lined with sheets; provided with sheets
examples: The roof was sheeted with metal panels.
They sheeted the windows with plastic for protection.

senses.definition: a device or person that sheets; someone who produces sheets of material or paper; in bakery, a device that flattens dough
examples: The newspaper press worker is a sheeter.
The bakery uses a sheeter to roll dough smoothly.

senses.definition: plural of sheeter; multiple devices or people that sheet
examples: The factory acquired two new sheeters.
They hired extra staff as sheeters during peak season.

senses.definition: printing or production using individual sheets of paper rather than a continuous roll
examples: Sheetfed printers are common for small runs.
They moved from roll-fed to sheetfed equipment.

senses.definition: a sudden abundance or excess of sheets or papers in a system or process
examples: The sheetflood overwhelmed the document management system.
During the audit, there was a sheetflood of records to review.

senses.definition: as much as a sheet can hold; a quantity measured in sheets
examples: He bought a sheetful of blank sheets.
A sheetful of paper weighs a few kilograms.

senses.definition: more sheet-like; having greater transparency or resemblance to a sheet
examples: This fabric is sheetier than that one.
The sheetier fabric lets in more light.
